reviews.review_description:
Pros: Amazing miniatures, lovingly crafted, easy on the queasy VR experience (largely static), Reaching through walls mechanic on Liz Borden murders was well implemented.  Lack of bloodshed was refreshing and probably mandated by App Store restrictions but I still felt like it was good.  When “red” is shown for blood it is abstracted as paint being added to the model which was tasteful.  

Cons:  Experience amounts to little more than assembling the model and hearing, tied to the placement of each house piece on a floor plan, a narration of what the significance of each room was.  Unfortunately for some rooms there was little to no real significance and so this approach is kind of lack luster.  If nothing happened in the back entry room (making up room name) then hearing something to the effect of “this is the back entry were the maid could come and go” does not really ad much. 

Given that it’s VR and immersive there was SO much missed opportunity in: 
- Storytelling
- Building tension 
- Putting viewers in the mind or the perspective of the victims or murders. 
- Perhaps solving something or at least attempting to assemble the house or pick a path through the house (like planning a heist/crime yourself), having been given clues about the significance of each piece to drop hints that, for example , “this might be the perfect point of entry because I learned that the maid went home early that night”
- Hands looking like insulation was weird at first because it was not explained why your hands had an overlay that looked like gloves but dirtier.  I later put it together that it was perhaps supposed to look like your hands were part of the wall space (still not 100% sure).  
- The mechanics of placing rooms could have used more explanation (it would have been important to say “let go of piece in approximate location and watch it snap into place”.  If that was said, my friend I showed Wonderland murders to completely missed that and took over 5 minutes of tinkering to realize “oh, I need to let go!”.  She was meticulously trying to fit the rooms in the correct orientation, etc. 

So if you are excited about True Crime, this might not be for you.  If you love hand crafted miniatures, maybe?   They are very cool in and of themselves.

Need more of this game I think they killed it with this it's a mix Reality of a crime podcast and 3rd models of scenarios that actually happens while we have a excellent narrator telling and showing g us what happened please give us more! 🙏
There is a charm that these dioramas tell a story with all that works in the details, waiting for new stories so keep it going

Please put on Amanda murders or Sharon Tate murder ￼ put on one of those stories. I don’t wanna hear about the Kardashian getting her jewel taken from her. That’s not crafting crimes to me anyway.  I am along with many others. Haven’t wait this long to see another crime story so that we look at the Kardashian getting their jewelry stolen.  Let them  go to the documentary on TV.  This is crafting crime. Please do not put  on the Kardashian’s.  Even though I know you may have to show it.  I’ll have to wait.  Along with the other who would like to see,  any of the other stories.  😢😠😴 ￼

developer_response.body:
Hi SweetMusic4me,
Thank you for sharing your thoughts with us, we’re really touched by your engagement and honest feedback. It’s incredibly valuable for us to know what our community wants to see, and we’ll share updates on this soon. For now, all we can say is that the vote is sparking a lot of different opinions, which is really interesting!
In the meantime, Episode 3, 'The Mona Lisa Heist', is now out, and we’re confident you’ll love it. Let us know what you think!
— The TARGO Team

reviews.review_description:
This is so good! The execution of this mixed reality crime podcast is perfect! The lightning and modelling of the 3D items you build up the crime scene, the shadow play of the victims and perpetrators , the sound design, the stereoscopic video of the presenter on the background. Everything is greatly crafted by some serious talents. So refreshing to see something this high quality on this platform. 

Will definitely buy some more episodes to consume in the future. 

The only feedback I have, is that I would like the ability to explore the miniature models after completion. Not just the full-size scenes. And maybe an option to change the height from the ground of everything. It’s not perfectly optimized for seated play as I need to hunch down before recentering to get a perfect angle.
Takes you in to the scene with the narration and into the story with the reconstruction. But why wouldn't you let me paint the BLOOD???

If you like entertaining true crime podcasts,  and you've been wondering how presenting those stories could work in VR, Crafting Crimes is certainly an answer. The stories are delivered with the polish and production of what you'd want from a modern NPR-style podcast: Perky millennial-sounding host, historical fact-finding,  morbid but non-gory details.  Everything's packaged around you, the player,  interacting with a diorama-style model of crime scenes and locations as the details of events are stitched together like the building blocks in your hands. 

It's all very pleasant and engaging. None of the eye-strain or movement issues that some people have with VR show up here. It's a seated experience where you're pretty much locked in place and play with the environments like Puzzling Places meets Cubism, presented like some guided museum tour with a really entertaining curator. 

If you're looking for something more passive than a 'game',  more interactive than watching a movie on a screen, and don't mind learning a little more about the darker and more sinister corners of human history, Crafting Crimes is a nice distraction.
That was cool

This is so interesting! I loved the whole presentation. The interactive nature was perfect. I did have the issue of hand tracking not always working, but as long as I kept both hands in front of the headset at all times, the issue mostly went away. I have only one request, allow a way for us to skip the video. I had to restart the experience a few times due to hand tracking issues, and I ended up having to watch the video all the way through every time. I would like to have ability to skip the video once I watched it all the way through once. Overall it was great though!

developer_response.body:
Hi GigiPug,

Thank you so much for this detailed feedback. We really appreciate you taking the time to share your experience!

We’re sorry to hear you had some trouble with hand tracking. Could you tell us a bit more about the issue you encountered? It would really help us investigate. Feel free to reach out directly at aurore@targostories.com so we can look into it with you.

Regarding skipping the videos: we currently have a checkpoint system in the experience that should allow you to resume close to where you left off. That said, some of the checkpoints may be placed slightly after the videos, which can make it frustrating if you need to restart. We’ll work on implementing checkpoints immediately after the videos to make sure you don’t have to watch them again.

Thanks again for your thoughtful feedback, we’re super glad you enjoyed the experience overall! 🙌

reviews.review_description: Icroyable
Love it but wish there we're humans and not shadows
Too short
Really enjoyed this experience. This is why I invested so much in vr early on. I hope the developer continues making more content 🙏

This fresh take on true crime podcast had me hooked right away. I thought it was put together well, enjoying the shadows and interactions throughout held my interest on the first episode. Maybe if we could have the opportunity to place the characters and weapons used at the right place in the scene as told by the narrative, would help with crime reconstruction. Being able to spray luminol for blood stains would be cool, giving us the character placement. Just small stuff like that for more interactions adding to placing furniture. I bought the second episode for the intrigue, however i will not purchase anything having to do with the Kardashians jewelry theft. I personally like the murder mysteries. Good job so far devs. What happened to being able to explore the scene when done?
This is an interesting way to learn about crimes from the past. Think of it as a podcast you can  see and interact with. I enjoyed my experience and am looking forward to more in the future.
Very good storys cant wait for more
So far some great stories to me the one i would like to see is the defeo murders in Amityville crazy story great build

reviews.review_description: the storytelling is so good and the model of the house but it feels so alive in a way that I cant explain but don't stop

I never expected a true crime story could blend with podcasts and transform into a mixed reality experience. The overall tone and atmosphere are spot-on, with carefully crafted spatial video that looks stunning.

As the narrative unfolded, I couldn’t help shouting “OMG… Don’t do that!”half expecting some gruesome imagery. Luckily, it was just my imagination running wild. Instead of graphic violence, characters are portrayed through shadows, which adds a dramatic touch without being gory. 

In the end, it’s simply brilliant. Are we listening to a podcast, or are we playing a game? The answer is: both! This fresh take on an interactive podcast feels truly innovative and honestly, it’s just so cool.
So bad to spend your money to play this game for episode 2

developer_response.body:
Hi ChrisWtk06,

We’re truly sorry to hear that Episode 2 didn’t meet your expectations. It’s always disappointing for us to know that someone didn’t enjoy the experience.

With Crafting Crimes, our goal is to create an immersive podcast series, offering an interactive take on the true crime genre. It is not designed as a game, and we understand that those expecting one may feel disappointed. We do our best to make this clear in the description so our audience know what kind of experience to expect.

We truly value your feedback and would love to understand more about what didn’t meet your expectations. If you’re open to it, please feel free to contact us at aurore@targostories.com — we’d really appreciate hearing from you!

Thank you for giving Crafting Crimes a try.
– The TARGO Team

reviews.review_description: I vote for the next episode whatever one you come up with it's going to be good

We can’t wait to discover which story the community wants next. Whatever wins, we’re already looking forward to bringing it to life for you.

Stay tuned 🙂

– The TARGO Team

reviews.review_description:
This is how they market it: “A true crime podcast, reimagined for mixed reality.” What it actually feels like: sitting through the world’s longest first-year detective class presentation.

The idea sounds incredible — building miniature crime scenes, exploring famous cases hands-on — but the execution is painfully dull. There’s no real interaction beyond placing a few objects while being talked at by a woman who might as well be reading her thesis aloud. Carol K. Ras, the host and “miniaturist,” never once draws you into the illusion. She just stands there, explaining things in a flat, Scholastic tone, like she’s grading herself.

For a mixed reality title, there’s shockingly little “reality” or “mix.” The set design is sterile, the engagement minimal, and the fashion choices distracting. If you’re going to cover Victorian murders or 1940s crimes, commit — dress the part, build the world. Give us atmosphere, not a TED Talk.

It’s a brilliant concept with lifeless execution. Thank God it was free — and thank God I could uninstall it as fast as I did.
If you like crime series, that one's a gem, I'm waiting for the next story to come up

Good news: Episode 3, The Mona Lisa Heist, is now out. We hope this new case keeps you just as hooked. 🎨
— The TARGO Team

reviews.review_description:
Loved the experince, its fun and interactive

loved the hand to disappear shader

So yes very interesting but I wish it was a little more interactive like paint the blood yourself or put together the walls yourself feel like they missed out on alot of things around the interactive aspect

developer_response.body:
Hi DenverStearns,

Thank you so much for sharing your thoughts, we really appreciate you taking the time to let us know how you felt about the experience!

With Crafting Crimes, our intention is to create an immersive, hands-on podcast experience that lets listeners physically engage with the story as it unfolds. That said, it’s not designed to be a fully interactive game.

We’re constantly reflecting on how to push the interactive dimension a bit further, and feedback like yours helps us better understand where expectations might go beyond what we currently offer.

A new episode is currently cooking! In the meantime, you can now vote in the app which episode you’d love to see us build next, right from the menu. We’d love for you to take part in the voting 🗳️ 

Thanks again for sharing your perspective, it truly helps us grow.

– The TARGO Team

reviews.review_description:
Finally, something new and interesting in VR. Not a kiddie meme game, no survival horror, not a buggy early access mess! This really showcases how storytelling can come to life in this medium. I loved the first episode and can’t wait for the rest. 

These Devs deserve support! I would gladly pay for this type of content. The VR ecosystem is getting a bit stale with the same old, same old. This type of experience is more adult themed versus kid focused. I enjoyed the mix of crafting the scene as the story unfolded in front of me. And the inclusion of the shadows and characters representing the people involved really brought it to life. 

Let’s support this type of app so more will be made. I hope some of the big VR blogs like Upload cover this game. It deserves more attention. Happy to pay for future DLC, Devs. Great work.

developer_response.body:
Hello BluSkyler!

Thank you so much for taking the time to write this. Reading feedback like yours truly means the world to us. 

Good news: a new house is already available in the app, and this is just the beginning. More stories are on the way 😉

Thank you sincerely for your support and encouragement, it helps us push this project further and keep creating the kind of VR experiences we all want to see more of!

— The TARGO Team

Hand tracking is non existent no

developer_response.body:
Hi GizmoDemi!
Thanks for your message. We’re sorry to hear you’re having trouble with the app.
Could you tell us a bit more about what happened so we can better help you?

The most likely cause is that hand tracking isn’t enabled on your Quest.
To turn it on, go to:
Library → Settings → Movement Tracking (left sidebar) → Hand & Body Tracking → On

This should resolve the issue and let you enjoy the experience!
If the issue persists, please email us at aurore@targostories.com, we’ll be happy to assist you further.
– The TARGO Team

reviews.review_description: cool game but i was hopping to be able to build my own crime scenes but instead it was basically just a true crime podcast the whole time

Do you like true crimes podcasts? Then this experience is a must "GET" as it brings the genre to life through the magic of Mixed Reality as you get to explore miniature dioramas of the crime scenes. It only has one episode currently (about the Lizzy Borden House murders), but two more are planned in the future. I hope they add the ability to freely explore the dioramas at eye-level in the future where we get to collect the evidence ourselves (think dusting for fingerprints, taking photos, etc.)

I was impressed with the originality of the experience. I enjoyed placing the rooms together, and furnishing them. On top of all of that, it was informative about crimes, which I had heard of, but knew little about.

Fine concept. Too bad that when I had to quit out of the game in the middle of the Lizzie Borden case, the game neither offered me an option to save my place/progress or even warmed me that I’d lose my progress through the episode if I quit out mid-episode. Just fired the app up,again, and it’s making me sit through the intro again. No thank you. Life is too short for that.

developer_response.body:
Hi Kvetchy_Kvaddle,

Thank you so much for taking the time to share this. We’re truly sorry for the frustration you experienced.

Your feedback helped us realize how important this was. We’ve now added a feature that allows you to save your progress at key moments during each episode, so you won’t have to start over again.

We really hope you’ll consider giving Crafting Crimes another try. A new episode is now available!

Thanks again for your helpful comment.

– The TARGO Team

reviews.review_description: If you like true crime, you will enjoy this little app for sure. Well made

I personally don't find the case available at the moment (Lizzie Borden) all that interesting, but the way this is presented is simply incredible. Fantastic production values, a great sense of how to use mixed reality and just a wonderful presentation. Despite not being all that interested in the subject matter, I was hooked from start to finish.

Even if you don't like true crime as a genre, or don't like this particular case, this is still worth trying out. It's just that well-made.
Super interesting new idea - i love the idea how a cool story is told. Fun to watch and feels much more captavating like just listening to a podcast
I found this to be very enjoyable and entertaining. It was also educational. Very pretty as well. I'm waiting for more crimes.

reviews.review_description: Great for true crime lovers
I played 1 level. It is so fun. Gotta play.

This was a wonderful way to experience a true story. Nice watching the story unfold as the viewer has a hand as the scene is crafted. Great narration and interesting approach to spaces highlight or change as the story is told.

Looking forward to more episodes. Would definitely purchase DLC.

As a museum director, I would love to see this approach used for museums and historic sites with curators crafting the space as they share the history, objects and stories. Especially important for sites lost to disasters, such as the 2023 Lahaina wildfire.
Sitting room......where
This is brilliant and very well done on the project, I’m very impressed with the team behind this one.. will buy more in the future.. 😍👍

reviews.review_description: The combination of the 3d cinema true crime podcast and the interactive building of the crime scene was an amazing experience.  This is hand tracking only, at first I was a bit disappointed because hand tracking is usually hit or miss with me but this was flawless. in this story you build the crime scene as a puzzle with pre-built rooms to the house and also placing some furniture in key rooms, while you are given descriptions of each room building the story while building the house.  The story telling was great, tje environment looked great, and the interactions kept you engaged in the story.  Well done experience hope to see more in the future.
Good

Ok, this was neat! As of now I can only go through the Lizzie Borden case, but would definitely pay a few dollars as more get added.  You place the miniature rooms of the house as the host (who recreates these mini crime scenes), narrates the details of the crime as you go, with sound effects and visual touches added to the miniature. It's cool seeing the shadows of the people on the diorama and it cuts open and zooms in showing the crime scene, but with corrugated cardboard walls, and giant looking craft tools beside you. It's interesting and perfect for a little (maybe 10 minutes?) VR experience.
I look forward to more and hope they add a bit more interactivity, such as being able to paint the blood ourselves instead of watching it just appear.

But this one is truly unique!! If you love true crime, podcasts, miniatures, or truly unique MR this is worth checking out! Right blend of creepy, storytelling and just so interesting! A bit of a slow start but it's worth it!
That was terrific. An imaginative and visually interesting way to learn about and experience new subjects. A good use of VR and MR.

more Targo Stories, please, I enjoyed this mixed reality experience

maybe future full immersive VR Stories also?
Personally, I'm not that excited about the topic, but the way it's presented is unique and highly polished. It's also free, so well worth checking out.
This is perfect for VR.  Visually pleasing, interactive, and captivating story.  i would be willing to pay a few $ for each of the next episodes.
This type of app is the reason I paid $300 dollars  to get the Meta quest. I love it, can’t wait!
This is one of the coolest things i downloaded. Fact. Its so clever 😀😀😀

developer_response.body:
Hi, thanks for your message—and I’m sorry about the trouble!

Our app is hand-tracking only, so the most likely cause is that hand tracking isn’t enabled on your Quest. To turn it on, go to:

Library → Settings → Movement Tracking (left sidebar) → Hand & Body Tracking: On

That should let you enjoy the experience! 

If the issue persists, please email victor@targostories.com.
